rulings.Case Title: WAN FEN TAN VS. RICARDO IAN SUMMERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: For Court To Appoint Contractor Pursuant To The Settlement Agreement Between Plaintiff And Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF WAN TAN FOR COURT TO APPOINT CONTRACTOR PURSUANT TO THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT is continued to August 30, 2024. No later than July 31, 2024 parties to file supplemental declarations each attaching 3 current bids for repairs addressing "cure of the violations" only as contemplated by the settlement agreement. The bids presented shall be valid at least until September 6, 2024 giving the parties sufficient time to accept the bid after the hearing. By no later than August 9, 2024 parties to file a joint supplemental brief advising the Court if the parties agreed on a contractor. The brief shall not to exceed one page in length. If parties cannot agree on a contractor, parties to submit separate declarations, not to exceed 1 page, reflecting their chosen contractor/bid. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. PLAINTIFF WAN TAN For Court To Appoint Contractor Pursuant To The Settlement Agreement Between Plaintiff And Defendant

rulings.Case Title: SIPING HONG VS. MEI YAO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANT YONG LAN DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to (1) clearly identify which cause of action is alleged against which defendant; (2) to allege facts (not conclusions) in support of each element of each cause of action alleged against Defendant Lan. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
